title: Air-fryer chicken nuggets
subtitle: Proper homemade nuggets with panko coating
excerpt: Chicken breast cut into bite-sized pieces, coated in seasoned flour, egg, and panko breadcrumbs, then cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes. Homemade nuggets have a noticeably better flavour than shop-bought because the chicken is real breast rather than reformed meat.
sourceNotes: Chicken nuggets were created by Robert Baker at Cornell University in the 1950s and popularised by fast-food chains from the 1980s. The UK air-fryer version replicates the crisp exterior and tender interior of the fast-food original using real chicken breast and a panko coating. Homemade nuggets lack the reformed-meat texture of the original but have better flavour.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze after coating, before cooking. Cook from frozen at 200°C for 15 minutes.

content.text: Cut the chicken into pieces of similar size. Nuggets that are noticeably different in size will cook unevenly: the small ones will overcook while the large ones are still finishing. Aim for pieces around 4 cm across and at least 1.5 cm thick.

Set up three shallow bowls: seasoned {{plain-flour}}, beaten {{eggs}}, and {{breadcrumbs-panko}} mixed with {{garlic-powder}}, {{onion-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Working in batches, coat each piece of {{chicken-breast}} in flour, then egg, then press into the panko to coat all sides. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Place in the basket in a single layer, not touching. Spray with cooking oil spray. Cook at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes, shaking the basket once at the halfway point, until golden and cooked through. Do not overcrowd: cook in two batches if needed.

items.symptom: Some nuggets still pink inside when others are golden outside
items.fix: Cut to a uniform 3 to 4 cm size. Alternatively, use a thermometer and check the largest pieces: 74°C internal means they are done
The oil spray is essential. Panko without fat cannot go golden in the air fryer. Spray both before and at the halfway flip for the best colour

items.cause: Underseasoned coating or very plain chicken breast
items.fix: Season both the flour and the panko layers, not just one. Add a small amount of fine salt directly to the beaten egg as well for full coverage

content.text: Homemade air-fryer nuggets take 25 minutes including prep and are significantly better than frozen. Make a large batch, freeze before cooking, and pull out a portion when needed: they cook from frozen in 15 minutes. Serve with ketchup, sweet chilli sauce, or honey mustard.
